title: Fixing a React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Nested Object References

statement.markdown: A memoized React component is causing unnecessary re-renders due to changing nested object references. The component is designed to update its state when a deeply nested object property changes. However, it's re-rendering excessively. Your task is to fix the bug by ensuring the component only updates when the actual nested object reference changes.
statement.inputFormat: An object with nested properties and a function to update the nested property.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the component should re-render.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the actual nested object reference changes.

examples.input: Object { a: { b: { c: 1 } } }, function updateProperty(obj, path) { obj.a.b.c = 2; return obj; }
examples.explanation: The nested object reference hasn't changed.

examples.input: Object { a: { b: { c: 1 } } }, function updateProperty(obj, path) { obj.a = { b: { c: 2 } }; return obj; }
examples.explanation: The nested object reference has changed.

hints.text: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare the previous and current object references.
Consider using a library like `lodash` to help with deep object comparison.

editorial.title: Fixing a React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Nested Object References
editorial.intuition: The component should only re-render when the actual nested object reference changes.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force approach is to use a recursive function to compare the previous and current object references.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare the previous and current object references.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected component will only re-render when the actual nested object reference changes.

testCases.input: Object { a: { b: { c: 1 } } }, function updateProperty(obj, path) { obj.a.b.c = 2; return obj; }
